title: Data Dictionary Template
content: Data dictionary fields: Field Name; Business Name (friendly label); Data Type (string/integer/decimal/boolean/date/datetime); Length/Precision; Constraints (nullable/unique/foreign key); Allowed Values (enum list or range); Default Value; Source System; Description (business meaning); Owner; Last Updated.

title: ETL Requirements Template
content: ETL requirements fields: Source System (name/type/connection); Source Query/File (extraction logic); Transformation Rules (field mapping/calculations/filters); Destination System (target table/schema); Load Strategy (full replace/incremental/upsert); Schedule (cron expression); Frequency (real-time/hourly/daily/weekly); Error Handling (reject file/skip/halt); Audit Logging.
tags: etl requirements template source transformation load

title: Reporting Requirements Template
content: Reporting requirements fields: Report Name; Audience (role/team); Frequency (on-demand/daily/weekly/monthly); KPIs/Metrics (with calculation formula); Dimensions (filters/groupings — region/period/product); Drill-down capability (level of detail); Data freshness requirement; Export format (PDF/Excel/CSV); Access control (who can view).

title: Data Quality Rules - 6 Dimensions
content: Data quality dimensions: Completeness (required fields populated — target >99%); Accuracy (values match source of truth); Consistency (same value across systems); Timeliness (data available within SLA after event); Validity (values conform to format/range rules); Uniqueness (no duplicate records). Each dimension needs metric and threshold.

title: Data Quality Rule Pattern
content: DQ rule template: Rule ID; Dimension (completeness/accuracy/consistency/timeliness/validity/uniqueness); Rule Statement (field X must not be null); Dataset/Table; Measurement SQL; Threshold (>99%); Failure Action (alert/reject/quarantine); Owner; Review frequency. Rules automated in data quality framework.

title: Master Data Management Requirements
content: MDM requirements: Golden record definition (authoritative source per entity); Match/merge rules (how duplicates identified and merged); Survivorship rules (which source wins for each field); Stewardship workflow (human review for unresolved merges); Distribution to consuming systems (real-time/batch); Hierarchy management (org/product/geography); Audit history of changes.

title: Data Lineage Documentation
content: Data lineage captures: Source (system/table/field); Transformation applied (formula/filter/join); Destination (system/table/field); Business rule applied; Tool/process that performs transformation; Frequency of data movement; Owner of each step. Enables impact analysis and compliance audit. Required for regulated data.

title: Dashboard Requirements Specification
content: Dashboard requirements: Purpose and audience; KPIs displayed (name/formula/target/threshold for RAG); Chart types for each metric (bar/line/pie/gauge — justify choice); Refresh frequency (real-time/15min/hourly/daily); Filter options (date range/region/department); Drill-down paths; Mobile responsive requirement; Access control; Export capability.
tags: dashboard requirements kpi charts filters specification

title: Data Warehouse vs Data Lake Requirements
content: Data Warehouse: structured data; schema-on-write; optimized for BI queries; star/snowflake schema; governed/curated; use for regular reporting and dashboards. Data Lake: raw + structured data; schema-on-read; flexible exploration; use for ML/advanced analytics/data science. Data Lakehouse combines both patterns.

title: Dimensional Modeling Requirements
content: Dimensional model requirements: Fact table (numeric measures + foreign keys to dimensions); Dimension tables (descriptive attributes — slowly changing dimensions type 1/2/3); Star schema (one fact + surrounding dimensions — simple queries); Snowflake schema (normalized dimensions — complex but space-efficient); Grain definition (lowest level of detail in fact table — mandatory to specify).

title: Data Governance Framework Requirements
content: Data governance requirements: Data ownership (business owner per domain); Data stewardship (operational responsibility); Data catalog (metadata repository — searchable); Data classification policy (public/internal/confidential/restricted); Data quality program; Master data management; Change control for data schemas; Compliance reporting capability.

title: Data Classification Requirements
content: Four classification levels: Public (no restrictions — marketing data); Internal (employee access — operational reports); Confidential (role-based access + encryption at rest — customer PII/financial data); Restricted (MFA + encryption + DLP + audit log + restricted export — PHI/PAN/credentials). Classification label applied at field level in data dictionary.

title: Data Retention Policy Requirements
content: Retention policy requirements: Data category (transactional/analytical/audit/log); Retention period (legal/regulatory/business need); Retention basis (legal obligation/legitimate interest/consent); Deletion mechanism (hard delete/soft delete/anonymization); Retention schedule in data dictionary; Legal hold process (override retention); Backup retention separate from live retention.

title: Real-time vs Batch Processing Requirements
content: Decision criteria: Real-time (latency <1 second; event-driven triggers; streaming — Kafka/Flink; use for: fraud detection/live dashboards/notifications). Batch (latency minutes to hours; scheduled; use for: ETL/reporting/billing). Micro-batch (near-real-time 1-60 min; Spark Streaming; use for: analytics aggregations). Document latency SLA per use case.
tags: real-time batch processing requirements latency streaming

title: Data Migration Requirements Checklist
content: Migration requirements checklist: source data profiled (quality/volume/anomalies); data mapping document complete; transformation rules documented and validated; migration strategy (big bang/phased/parallel run); rollback plan; data validation checks (record count/checksum/sample comparison); cutover plan; post-migration verification; legacy system decommission timeline.

title: BI Tool Requirements Specification
content: BI tool requirements: Self-service capability (business users create own reports without IT); Connectivity (data sources supported); Data volume capability (rows/GB tested); Visualization types (charts/maps/gauges/custom visuals); Collaboration (share/comment/alert); Scheduling (automated delivery); Embedding (in application/portal); Security (row-level security/AD integration); Mobile support.

title: Data Access Control Requirements
content: Data access requirements: Role-based access control (RBAC) at schema/table/row/column level; Row-level security (users see only their organization data); Column masking (PII masked for non-privileged roles — show last 4 digits only); Data sharing agreements documented before access granted; Access review quarterly; Emergency access procedure with audit.

title: KPI Requirements Specification Pattern
content: KPI specification: KPI Name; Business Question answered; Formula (numerator/denominator/calculation); Dimensions (how sliced — time/region/product); Data Sources (which systems/tables); Refresh frequency; Target value (green threshold); Warning threshold (amber); Critical threshold (red); Owner (accountable for KPI); Visualization type recommended.

title: Analytics Use Case Requirements Template
content: Analytics use case requirements: Business question; User persona (who uses this insight); Decision supported (what action does insight enable); Data inputs required; Calculation/model description; Output format (table/chart/prediction score); Confidence/accuracy requirement; Refresh frequency; Integration point (where insight surfaced — dashboard/email/API/app).

title: Data Schema Change Management
content: Schema change requirements: all changes version-controlled; backward compatible changes (add nullable column/add table) need migration script; breaking changes (drop column/change type) require major version + migration plan + consumer notification; schema registry for data contracts; automated schema validation in CI/CD pipeline.

title: Data Requirements Review Checklist
content: Data requirements review: all data sources identified with owner; data dictionary complete for all key entities; data quality rules defined with thresholds; retention policy assigned to each data category; access control requirements specified; data lineage documented for regulated data; reporting requirements have audience/frequency/KPIs; migration requirements include validation plan.

title: Slowly Changing Dimension (SCD) Requirements
content: SCD type requirements: Type 1 (overwrite — no history needed; use for corrections); Type 2 (add new row with effective dates — full history needed; most common for reporting); Type 3 (add previous value column — limited history; use when only current and previous matter). Specify SCD type per dimension in data dictionary.

title: Data Sharing Agreement Requirements
content: Data sharing agreement requirements: parties (provider and consumer); data categories and fields shared; purpose and permitted uses; transfer mechanism (API/SFTP/direct DB); encryption in transit and at rest; retention at consumer side; deletion on agreement termination; audit rights; breach notification obligation; GDPR/compliance clauses.

title: Observability Requirements for Data Pipelines
content: Data pipeline observability: pipeline execution log (start/end/records processed/errors); data quality metrics per run; SLA breach alerting (pipeline not completed by X time); lineage tracking for each pipeline run; anomaly detection (sudden volume drop/spike); dashboard for pipeline health; on-call rotation for P1 pipeline failures.
